subject: SCIENCE
title: Family Traits
objective: Compare inherited traits using fictional organisms.
introduction: Offspring can inherit traits but are not exact copies; traits can involve genes and environment. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Family Traits”.
A key word used in the lesson “Family Traits”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Offspring can inherit traits but are not exact copies; traits can involve genes and environment.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Offspring can inherit traits but are not exact copies; traits can involve genes and environment.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

options: Offspring may resemble parents without being exact copies.

feedback.correct: Correct: Offspring may resemble parents without being exact copies.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. Offspring are always exact copies. and Environment never matters. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.explanation: The correct response is “fur pattern in cartoon animals” because it follows the lesson explanation: Offspring can inherit traits but are not exact copies; traits can involve genes and environment.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Family Traits; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Family Traits.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
